events.title: Canada issues air quality warnings over US wildfire smoke after Trump tariff threat
events.description: Officials say smoke from US fires has crossed border days after US president accused Canada of improper fire management After days of acrid smoke from northern wildfires choking major US cities and inspiring Donald Trump to threaten Canada with tariffs, officials now say the reverse is also happening as parts of Canada issue air quality warnings caused by American fires to the south. Officials in the Canadian province of British Columbia warned in an alert on Saturday evening that smoke from US fires, spurred on by thousands of lightning strikes in the north-east, was creating smoky conditions as polluted air drifted north. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Canada issues air quality warnings over US wildfire smoke after Trump tariff threat

events.title: Expanded 2026 North American World Cup: Spain's 'diligent' performance to Messi's enduring legacy
events.description: Eve Irvine is pleased to welcome Robbie Knock, Lead Commentator and sports journalist. The 2026 FIFA World Cup will be remembered not only for Spain's triumph on the pitch but also for the way football became enmeshed in politics, entertainment, institutional scrutiny, and the closing chapter of one of the sport's greatest careers. Robbie Knock argues that Spain's victory was the product of tactical superiority rather than fortune, while acknowledging that controversial refereeing decisions complicated perceptions of the tournament. He also presents Lionel Messi's expected international farewell as the conclusion of a defining era in world football, emphasising that dignity in defeat may ultimately reinforce rather than diminish his legacy.

events.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Mary Honeyball, former Labour Member of the European Parliament from 2000 to 2019 representing London. She offers a cautiously optimistic assessment of the arrival of Andy Burnham to 10 Downing Street. She frames the moment less as a potential political reset after years of institutional fatigue. Mary Honeyball argues that the significance of the new premiership lies in the possibility of restoring public confidence, rebalancing the relationship between central and local government, and offering a more hopeful national narrative after the political and economic dislocation associated with Brexit.

events.title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
events.description: Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull. Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife was not a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu. Continue reading...

events.description: The Guardian goes on the ground with Brazilian forces fighting to eradicate mining that has destroyed protected areas of Indigenous land The jerry-built bordello at the foot of Vila Cururu, an illegal gold-mining settlement at the heart of Brazil’s most desecrated Indigenous territory, was a hive of depravity until just a few weeks ago. events.title: Donald Trump to impose 50% tariff on most Canadian goods, White House says
events.description: Turmoil likely as Trump officials say Canada unfairly discriminated against US autos, alcohol and dairy products Donald Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods in response to the country retaliating against previous US tariffs, the White House announced Monday, declaring Canada has unfairly discriminated against American cars, alcohol and dairy products. The tariffs will hit a wide range of products, the White House said, including wine, hockey sticks and cement. They also include goods previously protected from import taxes under the United States-Mexico-Canada (USMCA) agreement. Continue reading...

events.title: Antibiotic-resistant bacteria increasingly leaving children vulnerable to common infections, global study finds
events.description: Exclusive: Research spanning 82 countries finds antibiotic resistance increased in every region between 2004 and 2022 Harry Booth has had a rough run through Australia’s coldest months. The 13-year-old has been in and out of hospital with a range of common chest and urinary tract infections that his doctors found challenging to treat. Harry was born with kidney failure and began dialysis at six months old, before receiving a kidney transplant from his father at age four. The surgery saved his life but comes with a trade-off: he now needs to take medications that suppress his immune system to prevent his body from rejecting the kidney. Continue reading...

events.title: A former Ferrari boss has a blunt message: Your commute is more dangerous than his old job
events.description: Jean Todt spent decades around the deadliest motorsport on Earth. He was a rally co‑driver, then ran Ferrari’s Formula One team, then became president of world motorsport’s governing body. In his early years, a driver dying at the track barely made news.

events.title: Hunger deepens for displaced families in Sudan’s El Obeid
events.description: UN humanitarians working in war-torn Sudan on Friday highlighted rapidly growing needs among the more than 100,000 displaced people now sheltering in camps in the city of El Obeid.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hunger deepens for displaced families in Sudan’s El Obeid

events.title: FBI boss details how agency secured record-shattering FIFA World Cup and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Scores feared dead after passenger ferry capsizes off Guyana
events.description: Sixty-seven people have been rescued but the ferry is thought to have had more than 130 people on board.

events.title: Ryanair profits drop as Iran war puts off passengers and lifts fuel costs
events.description: The Irish airline said oil prices had increased costs meanwhile Brent crude surpassed $90.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ryanair profits drop as Iran war puts off passengers and lifts fuel costs

events.title: Iran condemns US attacks on civilian infrastructure
events.description: Iran says it has received mediation proposals from multiple parties and insists diplomatic efforts remain active.

events.title: Yemeni women support families through enterprise as economic crisis bites
events.description: Amid Yemen’s harsh economic crisis, securing viable employment has become increasingly difficult for men.

events.title: EU pauses methane penalties amid energy crisis, US pressure
events.description: The European Commission says waiving penalties will ensure the security of energy supply.

events.title: US judge orders pause on Paramount–Warner Bros merger
events.description: Paramount risks $650m quarterly fees if merger delays extend beyond September, as per the merger agreement terms.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US judge orders pause on Paramount–Warner Bros merger

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: Biodiversity fears as human-bred hybrid fish integrate into Philippines lake
events.description: Escaped flowerhorn cichlids are causing concern for native species and about parasites capable of infecting humans Escaped ornamental aquarium fish have integrated into a local ecosystem in the Philippines, but scientists say they may be threatening the native biodiversity of the lake. Flowerhorn cichlids – human-bred hybrid fish prized for their bright-gold colour and prominent head humps – are believed to have escaped from breeding facilities into Lake Sampaloc, which sits in a volcanic crater, during a typhoon. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Biodiversity fears as human-bred hybrid fish integrate into Philippines lake

events.title: The war with Iran is escalating. What next?
events.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Suzanne Maloney of the Brookings Institution about the U.S. war with Iran, the increase in hostilities and what it means for the wider region
events.ticker_label: 🪖 The war with Iran is escalating. What next?

events.title: How 'blue dot fever' is affecting concerts
events.description: Live music remains popular, but many concerts are struggling with unsold tickets and cancellations. While superstars fill stadiums, smaller venues and acts are facing a crisis.

events.title: Guyana: Dozens feared dead after ferry capsizes
events.description: Rescuers have been able to save 67 passengers so far. It is unknown exactly how many people were on board as authorities said the passengers' list is inaccurate.

events.title: Who is Khalil al-Hayya, Hamas’s new leader?
events.description: Hamas on Monday named Khalil al-Hayya, one of the founding members of the Palestinian militant group, as its new leader. Hayya is seen as a respected and pragmatic Hamas veteran “who doesn’t get carried away easily”. Last year, he survived an Israeli attack in Doha targeting the Hamas leadership which left several people dead, including one of his sons.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Who is Khalil al-Hayya, Hamas’s new leader?

events.title: France commemorates the Vel d’Hiv roundup after a Paris plaque honoring rescuers is vandalized
events.description: France has commemorated the Vel d’Hiv roundup, a mass arrest of Jews by French police in 1942

events.title: Haiti: Signs of hope despite gang violence and political uncertainty
events.description: For millions of Haitians, daily life has become a struggle to stay safe, find enough food and simply move around their own neighbourhood.

events.title: Peacekeepers step up support as families return to south Lebanon
events.description: With violence in south Lebanon significantly decreased since mid-June, UN peacekeepers there have returned to core functions such as protecting humanitarian aid and restoring critical infrastructure as families continue to return home.

events.title: Family identifies young woman found fatally shot in home owned by Rep Ayanna Pressley's husband
events.description: Family members identified the young woman found fatally shot inside a Boston property owned by Rep. Ayanna Pressley's husband as police continue investigating.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Family identifies young woman found fatally shot in home owned by Rep Ayanna Pressley's husband

events.title: Europe's 'green ideology' climate agenda risks crushing its industry, environment minister warns
events.description: Czech Environment Minister Igor Červený warns Europe will "crush" its industrial base unless leaders retreat from aggressive climate policies.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Europe's 'green ideology' climate agenda risks crushing its industry, environment minister warns

events.title: US service member killed in Iraq as troops recover unidentified remains in Jordan: CENTCOM
events.description: A U.S. service member was killed in Iraq while handling explosives from a downed Iranian drone as troops searching in Jordan recovered unidentified remains.
events.ticker_label: 💥 US service member killed in Iraq as troops recover unidentified remains in Jordan: CENTCOM

events.title: Incoming UK PM Andy Burnham rejects Thatcher-era policies, signals leftward shift
events.description: Andy Burnham condemned Margaret Thatcher's legacy and promised public ownership of utilities, housing and transport in his first Labour leader speech.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Incoming UK PM Andy Burnham rejects Thatcher-era policies, signals leftward shift

events.title: Burnham Advocates Closer EU Relationship: Big Gains Will Only Come from Rejoining the Single Market
events.description: Annette Young is pleased to welcome Tim Bale, a Professor of Politics at Queen Mary University in London. Andy Burnham inherits the Labour leadership at a moment when Britain's political debate is defined less by ideology than by structural constraints. Professor Bale argues that Burnham's success will depend on navigating three interconnected dilemmas: restoring economic growth to unlock fiscal capacity, rebuilding public confidence in the state's ability to manage migration, and gradually redefining Britain's post Brexit relationship with Europe without reopening politically divisive debates prematurely.

events.title: Man arrested after fire outside New York immigration court
events.description: A man has been arrested after setting off an incendiary device outside an immigration court building in New York City.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: Nicaragua’s president says country will not hold any more elections
events.description: Daniel Ortega, the 80-year-old authoritarian leader, says move will ‘build a wall’ against the opposition Nicaragua’s long-serving president, Daniel Ortega, has said the country will hold no further elections to prevent the opposition from coming to power. The 80-year-old authoritarian leader, who has been in office since 2007, also said he would work with the congress he controls to pass new laws that would “build a wall” against the opposition. Continue reading...

events.title: Mexican drug lord ‘El Mayo’ sentenced to life in prison in US federal court
events.description: Case against Ismael Zambada García, who helped co-found and lead the violent Sinaloa cartel, unfolded in New York The Mexican drug kingpin, Ismael Zambada García, known as “El Mayo”, was sentenced in a US federal court on Monday to life in prison, after a plea deal last year in which he admitted ⁠to shipping vast amounts of cocaine and other substances as leader of the violent Sinaloa cartel. Zambada is 76 and one of the most high-profile Mexican ⁠drug traffickers to be sentenced in a US court since his fellow Sinaloa leader Joaquín “ El Chapo ” Guzmán ​was sentenced to life in prison in ‌2019. The case unfolded in federal court in Brooklyn, New York . Continue reading...

events.title: Manufacturing union says Australia’s tax system is ‘rigged’ in bold manifesto ahead of Labor conference
events.description: AMWU calls for wealth and inheritance taxes, free education and more revenue from gas companies as it outlines ‘working-class agenda’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A new tax rate for the highest income earners, introducing wealth and inheritance taxes and extracting more revenue from gas companies form part of a new leftwing manifesto circulating before the Labor party’s national conference . The extra tax revenue could help raise the jobseeker rate, make education – including university – free, and nationalise aged care under what the Australian Manufacturing Workers’ Union (AMWU) calls a blueprint for a “working-class agenda”. Continue reading...

events.title: 'That is our challenge: to make politics work': Andy Burnham becomes UK PM
events.description: Andy Burnham has become Britain's new prime minister. The nomination process unfolded on July 20th in London, with one outgoing Labour leader passing the baton to another. The hope is that Burnham will ease the leadership turnover, stabilising the country.

events.title: What to know about Andy Burnham's first speech as prime minister
events.description: The UK has a new prime minister. Former Manchester mayor Andy Burnham is starting his premiership following Keir Starmer's resignation on July 20. In his first speech since officially becoming prime minister, he has promised a 10-year plan for the country. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot takes a look.

events.title: Chinese company demands compensation from the UK over British Steel nationalization
events.description: Jingye Group, the former owner of British Steel, has demanded compensation from the U.K. government for investment losses after the company's nationalization last week

events.title: Haiti: Training the next generation to fight corruption
events.description: How do you tackle corruption in a country grappling with insecurity, weak institutions and widespread impunity? In Haiti, the Government and the United Nations are betting on the country's youth.

events.title: Inside underground El Paso storm drain system Border Patrol says cartels exploit to smuggle illegal migrants
events.description: Border Patrol says 32 underground entry points from Mexico lead to exits in elementary schools, parks and backyards across El Paso neighborhoods.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Inside underground El Paso storm drain system Border Patrol says cartels exploit to smuggle illegal migrants

events.title: Tate brothers to remain behind bars for now as UK seeks extradition on rape, trafficking charges
events.description: Andrew and Tristan Tate will remain behind bars for now after appearing in a Miami federal courtroom as they begin fighting Britain's extradition request on 59 charges, including rape and human trafficking.

events.title: Moroccan-born man dies while detained by police in Italy
events.description: A video showing the final moments of Moroccan-born Abderrahim Fakir as he was detained by police has sparked protests.

events.title: Albanian protesters pelt lawmakers’ cars over resort projects
events.description: Protesters threw eggs at lawmakers' vehicles outside Albania's parliament, demanding changes to laws.

events.title: Houthis announce maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia
events.description: Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels have announced a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia after the two sides traded fire

events.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
events.description: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in which a court has ordered the work to be ⁠suspended. Construction continued, however, according to US officials and satellite imagery reviewed by Reuters. Continue reading...

events.title: England and Liverpool foortball legend Kevin Keegan dies aged 75
events.description: English former striker and manager Kevin Keegan has passed away at the age of 75 after a battle with cancer. He was a two-time European footballer of the year with Hamburg.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 England and Liverpool foortball legend Kevin Keegan dies aged 75

events.title: Russia says bus strike kills 5 in border region with Ukraine
events.description: The strike comes as Ukraine launched more than hundreds of drones at Moscow after a Russian missile barrage on Kyiv, as President Zelenskyy sought to calm growing tensions over Ukraine’s military leadership.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia says bus strike kills 5 in border region with Ukraine

events.title: India news: Police fire tear gas at 'Cockroaches' parliament march
events.description: Police had not granted permission for the march organized by India's Cockroach Janta Party movement. The CJP said its leader Abhijeet Dipke had been "picked up" by police but not "detained."

events.title: Lebanese army deploys to three southern pilot zones under US-backed security framework
events.description: The Lebanese army began assuming security responsibilities in three southern villages on Monday under a US-backed pilot initiative, the State Department said. Operations in Froun, Srifa and Zawtar al-Gharbiya mark the first implementation of a framework aimed at enabling a phased Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon and Hezbollah's disarmament.

events.title: US Virgin Islands' housing authority accused of corruption
events.description: The U.S. government says it has suspended funding for the U.S. Virgin Islands’ housing authority after an investigation found widespread corruption

events.title: Former China Development Bank president is under anti-corruption probe
events.description: China's top anti-graft watchdog has announced an investigation into a former president of China Development Bank

events.title: 'Today' suspect caught on camera before hate crime arrest, rap sheet reveals attempted kidnapping conviction
events.description: Andrew Truelove's criminal record spans two decades, including attempted kidnapping and making threats, before his alleged hate crime involving Craig Melvin.

events.title: Ortega insists no more elections for Nicaragua
events.description: The Central American state's longtime leader intends to block the opposition from challenging his rule.

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Putin meets North Korean foreign minister in diplomatic push
events.description: The visit by North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui comes without an obvious occasion, such as a multilateral diplomatic event or state anniversary, and has raised speculation that she may be coordinating a possible visit to Russia by North Korean leader Kim Jong Un.

events.title: 'If only people knew what a tick could do' - the deadly meat allergies caused by tick bites
events.description: Mammalian meat allergy is a growing problem along Australia's east coast - and it's caused by tick bites.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: ICE arrests human rights lawyer who fled Chinese crackdown
events.description: Arrest in Pennsylvania of Wu Shaoping, who is awaiting asylum decision, raises fears of deportation and persecution A Chinese human rights lawyer has been arrested by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), raising concerns he could be deported to China where he would face persecution. Wu Shaoping fled China at the end of 2019 amid a crackdown on human rights lawyers. He travelled to the US on a tourist visa and made an asylum claim in 2020, for which he is still awaiting a decision. Continue reading...

events.title: Moon Day focuses growing concerns around space junk
events.description: Amid surging lunar exploration, the UN on Monday urged governments to ensure that humanity's return to the Moon does not come at the expense of planet Earth – or the long-term sustainability of space.

events.title: Leaked Iran report finds record public anger as regime focuses on holding power
events.description: Iran's confidential report prepared for President Masoud Pezeshkian reveals majority support regime change or deep reform amid surging public anger over food prices and corruption.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: China's new AI model halts new subscriptions as demand swamps capacity
events.description: The Chinese AI model Kimi K3 has suspended new subscriptions after overwhelming demand pushed its capacity close to its limits

events.title: Tanzanian-flagged tanker from Yemen hijacked by suspected Somali pirates
events.description: A Tanzanian-flagged tanker was hijacked by suspected Somali pirates off Yemen’s coast, security sources and local officials familiar with the incident said

events.title: Woke language overhaul would strip the word 'inmate' from New York City's entire jail rulebook
events.description: The New York City Board of Correction is replacing "inmate" with "person in custody" across jail regulations as part of a person-first language effort.

events.title: Trump imposes 50% tariff on Canadian imports
events.description: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

events.title: Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia
events.description: The Houthis say the embargo begins immediately and is a response to a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Yemen.

events.title: Tate brothers appear in US court for extradition proceedings
events.description: They were arrested on Saturday after the UK announced dozens of new charges, including rape and sex trafficking, which they deny.

events.title: Notorious drug cartel leader Ismael Zambada sentenced to life in US prison
events.description: Known as "El Mayo", the co-founder the Sinaloa Cartel was also ordered to pay $15bn after he admitted to sending vast amounts of drugs to the US.

events.title: Rare identical quadruplets born in Australia
events.description: The parents of the four naturally-conceived girls were taken by "immense surprise" with the pregnancy, the hospital said.

events.title: Newcastle, Liverpool and England legend Keegan dies
events.description: Former England captain and manager Kevin Keegan dies at the age of 75, seven weeks after he revealed he had been diagnosed with stage four cancer.

events.title: JD Vance says wife Usha and son 'happy and healthy' after birth of fourth child
events.description: The couple welcomed their fourth child, Alec Neel Vance, on Sunday morning.

events.title: Food delivery rider wins China's top literary prize
events.description: Wang Jibing is among blue-collar Chinese workers gaining wider attention for their literary works.

events.title: Gaza residents weigh in on election of new Hamas leader
events.description: Gaza residents weigh in on election of new Hamas leader

events.title: What does Khalil al-Hayya’s election win mean for Gaza?
events.description: Al-Hayya wins Hamas leadership with a single-vote margin, signalling a focus on Gaza amidst continued Israeli aggression

events.title: Markets digest bank earnings after recent turmoil
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
events.description: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

events.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
events.description: This is it.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Australia spent more than $9m for each asylum seeker held on Nauru last year – so why can’t they afford to buy food?
events.description: Many of the more than 100 people awaiting processing on the tiny Pacific island are skipping meals to make ends meet, a new survey has found Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Most refugees and asylum seekers held in Nauru’s detention centres say they cannot afford to buy enough food or access clean drinking water, despite the Australian government spending the equivalent of more than $9m per person last financial year. The group of more than 100 people have been sent indefinitely to the tiny Pacific island while they wait for their asylum claims to be processed. Many of the cohort say they are skipping meals to make ends meet, according to a new survey by the Asylum Seeker Resource Centre (ASRC) advocacy group. Continue reading...

events.title: ‘One in 15 million’: Brisbane woman gives birth to identical quadruplets
events.description: Jenitar Na’amoana’s already had four children when she learned of surprise pregnancy ‘unheard of’ in Australia Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A 34-year-old woman from Brisbane has defied the odds and delivered four naturally conceived, identical quadruplets in what is believed to be an Australian first. Jenitar Na’amoana, who already had four children, was not planning having another but the quadruplets emerged from a “one in 15 million” pregnancy, splitting into four from a single fertilised egg. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 ‘One in 15 million’: Brisbane woman gives birth to identical quadruplets

events.title: India's Gen Z 'Cockroach People's Party' started as satire but is serious about change
events.description: They call themselves the "cockroaches" and they're on the march — demanding action against corruption, amid a shortage of opportunities for young people in India.

events.title: Andy Burnham becomes U.K. prime minister
events.description: In a transition of power presided over by King Charles, Andy Burnham has replaced Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Both are from the center-left Labour Party.

events.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s new prime minister?
events.description: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.

events.title: An Israeli paraglider tries to save migrating swifts that nest in the Western Wall
events.description: Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.

events.title: US imposes new 50% tariff on many Canadian goods
events.description: The tariffs will cover products including wine and hockey sticks, but not energy or fish. The move is the latest trade escalation between the US and its northern neighbor under the Trump administration.

events.title: AliExpress hit with record EU fine
events.description: The Chinese retailer has become the latest major online marketplace to face penalties under the EU’s Digital Services Act.

events.title: Guterres to visit Cyprus as UN seeks to revive peace talks
events.description: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres will visit Cyprus next week in what is seen as an effort to kickstart the stalled peace process. Why now? What is known about the plan? And what might be different this time round?

events.title: Why a 90-year-old treaty limits NATO in the Black Sea
events.description: A 90-year-old treaty still shapes the balance of power in the Black Sea. As Ukraine fights on against Russia, can NATO project power into waters it can't enter freely?

events.title: US ties to Israel rattle rare earth deal in Malaysia
events.description: Malaysia's rare earth refinery in Gebeng is essential to a strategic deal between the Pentagon and Australia's Lynas mining corporation. Now, Malaysian activists are using it as leverage to support Palestinians in Gaza.

events.title: Why Japan's new imperial law keeps women off the throne
events.description: New legislation aims to address the shrinking ranks of Japan's imperial family, while preserving male-only succession despite broad support for a female emperor.

events.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's prime minister
events.description: Andy Burnham has been sworn in as the successor to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. The former Manchester mayor has become a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

events.title: UK: Andy Burnham takes office as seventh PM in 10 years
events.description: After securing the leadership of the ruling Labour Party last week, Burnham has been invited to form a government by King Charles III. He promised both stability and major reforms in his first speech.

events.title: Trump signs order imposing new 50% tariffs on certain Canadian goods
events.description: US President Donald Trump signed orders on Monday imposing new 50% tariffs on some Canadian goods, citing what the White House called Canada's "discriminatory treatment" of US alcohol, automobiles and dairy products. The tariffs take effect in 30 days, with energy, potash and sector-specific imports exempted, the White House said.

events.title: Spain PM Sanchez visits Algeria for first time in four years as ties thaw
events.description: Spain’s Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez on Monday visited Algeria for the first time in four years in a bid to mend diplomatic ties between the two countries after they were frayed by a row over Western Sahara. In 2022, Madrid abandoned its neutrality stance over the disputed territory and backed Morocco’s sovereignty claim, angering Algiers. The thaw began last year, when Algeria reactivated a friendship treaty it had suspended over the dispute.

events.title: Senegal changes course to back ex-leader Macky Sall as UN chief
events.description: The Senegalese government on Monday made a U-turn, announcing it would back former president Macky Sall’s candidacy to replace UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres when his term expires. The government had previously declined to throw its weight behind Sall, whom it accuses of deadly political repression while in office, but reversed course after he paid the sitting president a visit in Dakar this weekend.

events.title: 'Labour thinks it has responsibility to prevent the rise of Reform UK', historian says
events.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Martin Farr, Senior Lecturer in Contemporary British History at Newcastle University, explains that "the Labour Party members and its MPs think that it's their responsibility to prevent" the rise to power of right-wing populist party Reform UK, and that has motivated the decision to replace Keir Starmer with Andy Burnham at 10 Downing Street.

events.title: Two French diplomats subjected to ‘extremely serious act of intimidation’ in Iran, FM Barrot says
events.description: France’s Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot on Monday said two French embassy officials had been subject to an “extremely serious act of intimidation” by Iranian security services in Iran the day before, having been held and interrogated for hours. One of them was also subjected to physical abuse, he said.

events.title: Andy Burnham: Who is the new UK prime minister?
events.description: 🇬🇧 Andy Burnham succeeds Keir #Starmer as UK Prime Minister. The former #Manchester mayor has pledged to tackle the cost of living, expand devolution and reform the social care system.

events.title: After getting a new PM, the UK waits for his government and first policies
events.description: Former Manchester mayor Andy Burnham has become the UK's new prime minister, the seventh in a decade, following Keir Starmer's resignation on July 20. In his first speech, he has vowed to start a ten-year plan for Britain. His government and his first policies will quickly be announced. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot tells us more.

events.title: Mexican drug lord sentenced to life in prison
events.description: Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada, a Mexican drug lord who trafficked substances into the US for the Sinaloa cartel, has been jailed for life.

events.title: US pledges to crack down on Haitian gangs making money off social media
events.description: The U.S. envoy to the United Nations says the U.S. government plans to stop violent gangs in Haiti from profiting off social media

events.title: Man charged with murder of former UK politician Ann Widdecombe
events.description: British prosecutors have charged a 28-year-old man with murder.

events.title: Hungary probes deal with China's BYD carmaker after ex-foreign minister takes job there
events.description: Hungary's government is investigating a major foreign investment by Chinese automaker BYD

events.title: Guyana launches criminal probe as crews scour waters for survivors after ferry capsizes
events.description: The search for survivors continues in Guyana after a ferry believed to be overloaded capsized over the weekend

events.title: Yemen's Iranian-backed Houthis say they will block Saudi shipping at Red Sea gateway
events.description: Yemen's Iranian-backed Houthi rebels have announced a maritime embargo against Saudi Arabia

events.title: Philippines condemns Chinese coast guard for injuring Filipino sailor
events.description: The Philippine military says Chinese coast guard personnel aboard a motorboat repeatedly struck a Filipino sailor on the head with a wooden baton in a disputed shoal in the South China Sea, injuring him and damaging his smaller vessel

events.title: 'King of the North' Andy Burnham went from Manchester mayor to Britain's new leader
events.description: Andy Burnham is about to become Britain’s 59th prime minister, following the sudden downfall of Labour Prime Minister Keir Starmer

events.title: Andy Burnham will become UK prime minister after a meeting with King Charles III
events.description: Andy Burnham is set to become the U.K.'s new prime minister on Monday, succeeding Keir Starmer

events.title: India police use tear gas amid march on Parliament by Cockroach movement supporters
events.description: Police in India have used tear gas and batons to stop supporters of India’s youth-led Cockroach movement from marching to Parliament

events.title: Baristas behind bars: Thai prisoners brew a path back to daily life
events.description: It looks like a regular coffee shop, albeit set up in an old red bus. Stairs lead up to a serving hatch, with a menu of drink options. A selection of jaunty coffee-related messages like “My one and only love: Americano” and “Miss you a latte,” adorn the side of the bus.

events.title: Guterres: AI must be shaped by 'all of humanity', not a handful of powers
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Friday urged governments and technology companies to work together to ensure artificial intelligence (AI) benefits all countries.

events.title: Breaking the silence: Zimbabwean youth leader calls for urgent investment in mental health
events.description: For Tanatswa Amanda Chikaura, mental health advocacy began with a personal loss: while studying psychology at the University of Zimbabwe, she learned that a fellow student had died by suicide.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Breaking the silence: Zimbabwean youth leader calls for urgent investment in mental health

events.title: Repeat offender busted again less than 24 hours after release in brazen jewelry store theft case: police
events.description: Surveillance video captures a California teen allegedly stealing jewelry from a store less than 24 hours after his jail release on identical charges.

events.title: Jiu-jitsu instructor and pastor accused of horrific crimes against children in exclusive California enclave
events.description: Bail was set at $17.5 million for Emmanuel Marchica, a Hollister pastor accused of years of child sexual abuse across multiple community locations.

events.title: 'Long Island Lolita' Amy Fisher's son charged as cops probe possible microwave link in cat's killing
events.description: Brett Bellera, son of Amy Fisher, and his girlfriend face felony animal cruelty charges after their cat allegedly died from extreme heat exposure.
events.ticker_label: 💥 'Long Island Lolita' Amy Fisher's son charged as cops probe possible microwave link in cat's killing

events.title: More than 100 Haitian immigrants with violent offense and sex crime convictions deported: DHS
events.description: A July 16 deportation flight to Haiti carried over 100 illegal immigrants with convictions for child molestation, armed carjacking, and drug trafficking.
events.ticker_label: 💥 More than 100 Haitian immigrants with violent offense and sex crime convictions deported: DHS

events.title: NORAD F-16s intercept multiple aircraft violating temporary flight restrictions over New Jersey
events.description: More than 10 general aviation aircraft entered restricted airspace over Bedminster and East Rutherford in New Jersey, with NORAD F-16s deploying flares.

events.title: Bulldog's drowning sparks police probe at dog resort marketed as the 'Disneyland for dogs'
events.description: Bruce, a 1-year-old English bulldog, allegedly drowned at Camp Canine in Santa Barbara after ending up in a large-dog yard with unfenced pools.

events.title: Andy Burnham takes over as UK prime minister, calls for 'new political model'
events.description: Andy Burnham enters Downing Street vowing to shift power away from London, but his past criticism of Trump could create early friction with Washington.

events.title: Steve Forbes urges Trump to keep the Strait of Hormuz open at all costs
events.description: Publisher Steve Forbes calls for maximum pressure on Tehran regime, urges Trump administration to keep the Strait of Hormuz open as a non-negotiable imperative.

events.title: Trump administration touts more than $1B in UN budget cuts as reform push gains momentum
events.description: The Trump administration says it secured over $1 billion in U.N. budget cuts in six months, including a $550 million peacekeeping budget reduction.

events.title: Asian nation with 1,500-year-old imperial line insists only men can become emperor in policy revision
events.description: Japan's Parliament revised the Imperial House Law to cement male-only succession, blocking Princess Aiko from inheriting the throne despite public support.

events.title: Resource-rich nation praises US ties amid Washington-Beijing critical minerals race
events.description: Foreign Minister Thérèse Kayikwamba Wagner says the Democratic Republic of the Congo needs multiple partners to develop its cobalt, copper and lithium wealth amid growing U.S. interest.

events.title: African growth boom follows Trump push to replace aid with trade
events.description: Despite predictions of collapse after USAID cuts, sub-Saharan Africa is outpacing Asia in growth forecasts, fueled by trade and private investment.
